member.name: T:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadComponent
member.summary: Represents a road component that can be manipulated within the editor and at runtime.

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadComponent.IsLocked
member.summary: This will prevent the bridge from being rebuilt if any property is edited or if the road component get disable and re-enabled. Really useful if you plan to edit the mesh with the mapping tool so you don't accidently erase/rebuild the bridge.

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadComponent.BridgeMaterial
member.summary: This is your bridge material you wanna use. I recommend using a tileable texture for better result.

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadComponent.BridgePillarsKeepVertical
member.summary: Does the pillars follow world up vector or follow the road shape ?

member.name: M:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadComponent.AdaptTerrainToRoad
member.summary: Adapts the terrain geometry to the spline shape.

member.name: M:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadComponent.PaintTerrainToRoad
member.summary: Applies the materials to the terrain based on the road shape and the blend gradient.

member.name: T:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadIntersectionComponent
member.summary: Represents a road intersection component allowing you to select specific exit points

member.name: F:RedSnail.RoadTool.DrivingSide.Right
member.summary: Drive on the right side (USA, Europe, etc...)

member.name: F:RedSnail.RoadTool.DrivingSide.Left
member.summary: Drive on the left side (UK, Japan, etc...)

member.name: F:RedSnail.RoadTool.TrafficLightSystem.European
member.summary: Traffic lights placed near the intersection (before crossing)

member.name: F:RedSnail.RoadTool.TrafficLightSystem.US
member.summary: Traffic lights placed far from the intersection (after crossing)

member.name: F:RedSnail.RoadTool.IntersectionShape.Rectangle
member.summary: Rectangle mode is allowing you to toggle between 4 differents road exits

member.name: F:RedSnail.RoadTool.IntersectionShape.Circle
member.summary: (WIP) Circle mode is incomplete and really experimental yet

member.name: T:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent
member.summary: Generates parking lot lines for parking spaces

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.LinesMaterial
member.summary: The material used to render the line segments between each parking spots

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.LinesCap
member.summary: Do we want line caps at the start and end of the parking lot ?

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.LinesWidth
member.summary: The width of a line segment.

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.LinesOffset
member.summary: The height offset above the ground (This is used to avoid Z fighting with the ground)

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.LinesTextureRepeat
member.summary: How many units before the line texture repeat itself (This is used to avoid stretching)

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.SpotPrefab
member.summary: An optional prefab, if non-empty the parking lot will generate a bunch of child gameobjects positioned at each parking spots center. (e.g. this allows you to use a gameobject prefab with a car spawner system component attached to it)

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.SpotCount
member.summary: The amount of parking spots you want to generate.

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.SpotLength
member.summary: Well that's the parking spot length

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.SpotWidth
member.summary: and width...

member.name: P:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.SpotAngle
member.summary: The angle of the parking spots in degrees (0 = perpendicular, 45 = angled, 90 = parallel)

member.name: M:RedSnail.RoadTool.RoadParkingLotComponent.SnapToGround
member.summary: Utility button to directly snap the parking lot to the nearest solid ground

member.name: M:RedSnail.RoadTool.SandboxUtility.ShowEditorNotification(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Yes I know this is not intended to be used for notification purpose, but it does the job of notifying the user, so it's a nice work around until Facepunch give us a proper way to show editor notifications easily (Technically it's related to ToastManager class but only available on the editor side)
